summary: Golimumab has not been formally studied nor labeled specifically for sarcoidosis but may have effectiveness when used off-label in refractory cases with severe symptoms such as ocular or skin involvement due to its mechanism targeting tumor necrosis factor alpha—a pathway implicated in inflammation within sarcoidosis pathophysiology. This use remains investigational rather than established through large-scale trials; however, existing data support potential benefit among select patient populations who do not respond adequately to conventional treatments.
